note: OCG Standard §25 ocg-private-input@1. policy_parameters carries ONLY the sha256-salted@1 commitment to the private party list — never the parties themselves. Disclosure material (salt + plaintext party list) for these vectors lives OUT-OF-BAND in art-413-screen-sanctions-private.disclosure.json (test-only, never shipped as a live artifact field). Vector 1: clean list, 0 hits. Vector 2: list containing one OFAC-SDN-test hit.

vectors.name: clean_list_zero_hits
vectors.description: Private party list of 2 legitimate counterparties (disclosed out-of-band for this test vector). Zero OFAC-SDN-test hits -- clean verdict.

policy_parameters.list_source: OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) List test fixture set, per FinCEN 31 CFR Chapter X screening obligations. Production deployments substitute the live OFAC SDN + FATF Travel Rule party lists; this profile proves the SCREENING RAN against a committed list, not the list contents.

output_payload.regulatory_basis: FinCEN 31 CFR Chapter X OFAC/SDN screening obligation; FATF Recommendation 6 (targeted financial sanctions). Verdict proves the committed party list was screened against the pinned list version — the parties themselves are never disclosed (OCG Standard §25 ocg-private-input@1).
output_payload.pii_note: ZERO PII disclosed: the screened party/transfer list is a private witness, never present in policy_parameters or output_payload. Only the screening verdict (hit count, clean flag) is public.
output_payload.not_legal_advice: Not legal advice. Sanctions screening determinations require review by a qualified compliance officer against the live OFAC SDN list and applicable BSA/AML program.

vectors.name: one_sdn_hit
vectors.description: Private party list of 2 counterparties (disclosed out-of-band), one matching an OFAC-SDN-test name. hit_count=1, clean=false.

policy_parameters.list_source: OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) List test fixture set, per FinCEN 31 CFR Chapter X screening obligations. Production deployments substitute the live OFAC SDN + FATF Travel Rule party lists; this profile proves the SCREENING RAN against a committed list, not the list contents.

Verdict proves the committed party list was screened against the pinned list version — the parties themselves are never disclosed (OCG Standard §25 ocg-private-input@1).
